confidence_explanation: The evidence presents a clear, sequential attack chain by a user with 'read_only' privileges who successfully escalated to 'admin', accessed cross-departmental sensitive data (HR salary bands), exfiltrated data to an external IP, and performed anti-forensic actions (log deletion). The activity occurred off-hours from an anomalous IP address, and the metadata explicitly describes the log deletion as an attempt to hide the login event. There is no evidence of legitimate maintenance justification (e.g., ticket numbers) or privilege reversion.

attack_chain.description: Initial unauthorized access: user_04 (read_only) downloads confidential finance payroll data from an anomalous IP (185.220.101.34) during off-hours.
Continued data collection: user_04 downloads confidential finance contracts from the same anomalous IP.
Privilege Escalation: user_04 successfully changes their own role from 'read_only' to 'admin' (self-escalation), enabling further unauthorized actions.
Lateral Movement: user_04 accesses the /data/hr/ directory, which is outside their normal department scope (Finance).
Data Exfiltration (Cross-Dept): user_04 downloads sensitive HR salary data (salary_bands.xlsx) immediately after privilege escalation.
Anti-Forensics: user_04 deletes the authentication log (/var/log/auth.log) to cover tracks, with metadata explicitly noting the intent to hide the login event.
Data Exfiltration Confirmation: Large outbound data transfer (10MB) from the internal server to the external attacker IP (185.220.101.34).

gaps: No explicit login event (evt_s9_000) is visible in the timeline, likely due to the log deletion in evt_s9_007, though the session_id in subsequent events confirms a successful session was established.
No ticket number or maintenance window justification is present in the metadata for any of the actions.
narrative: user_04, a junior accountant with read-only access, executed a successful insider threat attack from an external IP during off-hours. The user escalated their privileges to admin, accessed and downloaded sensitive data from both Finance and HR departments, exfiltrated the data, and deleted authentication logs to cover their tracks.
